text: My friend and I listened to How to Be a Human Being in the car a few weeks ago and I have had the album on loop ever since. I'd really like to find more music with a similar sound. I love the way the vocals and instrumentals are arranged and produced, especially in "Life Itself," "Youth," "Pork Soda," "Mama's Gun," and "Poplar St." However, it's so distant from the type of music I normally listen to that I'm not sure where to start. Do you guys have any recs?
label: Critique
notes: Edge case: framed as a recommendation request but names specific songs as production examples; tie-break rule applied (specific claim overrides question frame).

text: Crooked Colours - Vera. Still Woozy - If This Isn't Nice, I Don't Know What Is. Goth Babe - Lola. Golden Vessel's albums. I'd also recommend exploring some of Dominic Fike, Everything Everything, Magdalena Bay, Villagers, and Balu Brigada.
